sast_review.reason: ET.fromstring() is used to parse untrusted XML without disabling DTD processing or external entity resolution. Python's xml.etree.ElementTree is known to be vulnerable to XXE by default. No defusedxml or safe parser configuration is applied.
Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without using defusedxml or calling defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() leaves the application vulnerable to XML attacks including XXE.

raw.summary: The code uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring which does not disable external entity resolution by default, making it vulnerable to XXE attacks (CWE-611). The code also fails to escape user-supplied name and avatar values before rendering HTML, leading to stored XSS (CWE-79). Both SAST findings are true positives.
